content:
CORS stands for **Cross-Origin Resource Sharing**.

It's a browser security mechanism (enforced via HTTP headers) that controls whether a web page running at one origin (domain/protocol/port) is allowed to access resources from a different origin. It's the modern, flexible successor to the strict "same-origin policy" — instead of blocking all cross-origin requests, the server explicitly declares which origins may read its resources via headers like `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`.
